title: Smoky Light Slovakian Pork Soup
description: A fragrant, smoky pork soup enriched with savory romano pepper, tangy pickles, and fresh vegetables-a comforting yet light Slovakian staple.
instructions: Heat olive oil in a large pot over medium heat.
Add pork cubes and brown on all sides, about 5-7 minutes.
Add diced onion, carrots, and celery; cook until vegetables soften, about 5 minutes.
Stir in minced garlic, smoked paprika, and romano pepper; cook for 1 minute until aromatic.
Pour in water, add bay leaf, and bring to a gentle boil.
Reduce heat and simmer, partially covered, for 45 minutes or until pork reaches 63 °C internal temperature and is tender.
Add diced pickles and cook for an additional 5 minutes to warm through.
Remove bay leaf, season the soup with salt and black pepper to taste.
Let the soup sit for a few minutes before serving to allow flavors to meld.
Serve hot, garnished with a sprinkle of fresh dill if desired.
